channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: "Pak Army Fought For Hafiz Saeed, Masood Azhar": Big Revelation On 2025 War

media:content.media:description: The PNP leader also admitted to meeting LeT founder Hafiz Saeed.

item.description: This admission tears into Islamabad's "non-state actor" facade and how Islamabad has been providing institutional support to terror networks under the guise of "freedom fighting".
item.content:encoded: This admission tears into Islamabad's "non-state actor" facade and how Islamabad has been providing institutional support to terror networks under the guise of "freedom fighting".

item.title: Elon Musk Wanted $80 Billion To Colonise Mars: OpenAI President At Trial

media:content.media:description: Elon Musk alleges that he was conned by OpenAI chief Sam Altman.

item.description: The trial, in its second week in a California courtroom, could determine the future of OpenAI, which is know for its launch of ChatGPT chatbot.
item.content:encoded: The trial, in its second week in a California courtroom, could determine the future of OpenAI, which is know for its launch of ChatGPT chatbot.

item.title: Gasoline Costs 50% More In The US Than It Did Before Iran War

media:content.media:description: Less oil on the market means higher prices for oil and gasoline.

item.description: The price of crude oil, which is the main ingredient in gasoline, has been climbing for most of the past two months.
item.content:encoded: The price of crude oil, which is the main ingredient in gasoline, has been climbing for most of the past two months.

item.title: Inside Cruise Ship At The Center Of A Deadly Hantavirus Outbreak

media:content.media:description: WHO investigates possible human transmission and suspects initial infection pre-boarding.

item.description: The MV Hondius, a Dutch ship on a weekslong polar cruise, departed April 1 from Argentina for Antarctica and several islands in the South Atlantic.
item.content:encoded: The MV Hondius, a Dutch ship on a weekslong polar cruise, departed April 1 from Argentina for Antarctica and several islands in the South Atlantic.

item.title: Cruise Ship Hantavirus Outbreak: Rare Human-To-Human Transmission Suspected, Says WHO

media:content.media:description: The hantavirus outbreak on MV Hondius has left 3 dead so far

item.description: A WHO update suggests rare human-to-human hantavirus transmission may have occurred on the cruise ship where an outbreak has left 3 dead so far. Risks to public health, however, still remains low.
item.content:encoded: A WHO update suggests rare human-to-human hantavirus transmission may have occurred on the cruise ship where an outbreak has left 3 dead so far. Risks to public health, however, still remains low.

item.title: Pakistan Navy Helps Stranded Indian Ship In Arabian Sea After SOS Message

media:content.media:description: The navy was assisted by Pakistan Maritime Security Agency in the rescue ops (Representative Image)

item.description: The Pakistan Navy launched a rescue and support operation for the crew-- six Indians and one Indonesian-- after the Maritime Rescue and Coordination Centre in Mumbai contacted authorities in Islamabad.
item.content:encoded: The Pakistan Navy launched a rescue and support operation for the crew-- six Indians and one Indonesian-- after the Maritime Rescue and Coordination Centre in Mumbai contacted authorities in Islamabad.

item.title: No Mail, No Passage: Iran Launches New System To Regulate Hormuz Shipping

media:content.media:description: Trump said the US would begin escorting ships out of the Strait of Hormuz, a plan he later cancelled.

item.description: Iran's parliament is considering legislation that would formalise restrictions on vessels tied to Israel and the United States.
item.content:encoded: Iran's parliament is considering legislation that would formalise restrictions on vessels tied to Israel and the United States.

item.title: US To Close Peshawar Consulate Citing Safety Of Diplomatic Personnel

media:content.media:description: The statement added that while the physical presence in Peshawar is being reduced

item.description: US closes Peshawar consulate, transfers diplomatic duties to Islamabad embassy.
item.content:encoded: US closes Peshawar consulate, transfers diplomatic duties to Islamabad embassy.

item.title: US Unveiled Plan To Reopen Hormuz Hours Before Trump Shelved It

media:content.media:description: Project Freedom was meant to sit at the center of the next phase of the US approach to Iran.

item.description: That decision - which Trump said he made at the request of mediator Pakistan - suggested the president didn't want to risk another flareup in violence.
item.content:encoded: That decision - which Trump said he made at the request of mediator Pakistan - suggested the president didn't want to risk another flareup in violence.

item.title: Companies Must "Reconceptualise Work" In AI Era: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella
item.description: Microsoft said that most organizations are still not built to fully use the benefits of this "expanded human ability" that comes with AI.
item.content:encoded: Microsoft said that most organizations are still not built to fully use the benefits of this "expanded human ability" that comes with AI.

item.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran, China Envoys Meet As Trump Pauses US Effort In Hormuz
item.description: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.

item.title: One Of The World's Largest Cities Is Sinking So Fast It's Visible From Space

media:content.media:description: NISAR mapped Mexico City's subsidence in detail.

item.description: One of the world's largest cities is sinking
item.content:encoded: One of the world's largest cities is sinking

item.title: Iran To Use 'Kamikaze Dolphins' To Attack US Ships In Hormuz? What Team Trump Said

media:content.media:description: CNN reported that the US military isn't using dolphins as part of its efforts to reopen Hormuz

item.description: The idea was first reported by the Wall Street Journal on April 30, with an article claiming that Iranian officials had said Iran could use "mine-carrying dolphins" to attack US warships.
item.content:encoded: The idea was first reported by the Wall Street Journal on April 30, with an article claiming that Iranian officials had said Iran could use "mine-carrying dolphins" to attack US warships.

item.title: US Woman Convicted Of Poisoning 4-Year-Old With Whiskey, Causing Her Death

media:content.media:description: An autopsy showed China's blood alcohol content was 0.680.

item.description: The woman is guilty of manslaughter in the 2022 death of China Record and faces up to 40 years in prison. Sentencing is scheduled for August 10.
item.content:encoded: The woman is guilty of manslaughter in the 2022 death of China Record and faces up to 40 years in prison. Sentencing is scheduled for August 10.

item.title: On Pakistan's Request, Trump Briefly Pauses Project Freedom, Blockade To Continue

media:content.media:description: Marco Rubio said a ceasefire in the Middle East was still in place

item.description: Trump said he was pausing the 'Project Freedom' for a short period to give space for US efforts to finalise a settlement with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Trump said he was pausing the 'Project Freedom' for a short period to give space for US efforts to finalise a settlement with Iran.

item.title: Putin Living In Bunkers As Kremlin Fears Drones, Coups And Assassination: Report

media:content.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is reportedly spending extended periods in underground bunkers.

item.description: Stricter controls, internet shutdowns and heightened protection for military elites reflect mounting anxiety within the Kremlin, even as online criticism and public discontent in Russia become increasingly visible.
item.content:encoded: Stricter controls, internet shutdowns and heightened protection for military elites reflect mounting anxiety within the Kremlin, even as online criticism and public discontent in Russia become increasingly visible.

item.title: Trump, Xi Jinping Likely To Discuss Taiwan Next Week

media:content.media:description: Trump is set to visit Beijing on May 14 and 15.

item.description: Taiwan is likely to be a topic of conversation between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping when they meet next week, said Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
item.content:encoded: Taiwan is likely to be a topic of conversation between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping when they meet next week, said Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

item.title: Trump Says Pausing US Operation To Help Ships Leave Hormuz

media:content.media:description: Trump said that "Project Freedom will be paused for a short period of time".

item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he was halting the US military operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz after just one day, in a bid to reach a deal with Iran to end the Middle East war.
item.content:encoded: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he was halting the US military operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz after just one day, in a bid to reach a deal with Iran to end the Middle East war.

item.title: OpenAI President Claims Elon Musk Physically Threatened Him

media:content.media:description: OpenAI President Greg Brockman said Elon Musk "was going to hit him".

item.description: According to Musk, the company pivoted to a for-profit structure and misappropriated his $38 million founding donation to build a company now valued at more than $850 billion.
item.content:encoded: According to Musk, the company pivoted to a for-profit structure and misappropriated his $38 million founding donation to build a company now valued at more than $850 billion.

item.title: Marco Rubio Says Offensive Action Against Iran Is Over

media:content.media:description: Rubio accused Iran of "holding the world's economy hostage" through threats to shipping.

item.description: The United States has completed its offensive operations against Iran, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday, even as Washington warned it was ready to unleash a "devastating" response to any new attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: The United States has completed its offensive operations against Iran, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday, even as Washington warned it was ready to unleash a "devastating" response to any new attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
